summary: The diff shows that REQ-1, REQ-2, and REQ-3 are fully implemented with six new security icons added to the toolbar using Lucide React components, drag-and-drop functionality implemented, and icon type definitions updated. However, REQ-4 regarding integration with AttackPathCanvas functionality (selection, movement, connection, save/load, undo, delete, multi-select) cannot be fully verified as the AttackPathCanvas component itself is not included in the diff.

missing_requirements.requirement: Ensure dragged security icons integrate with existing AttackPathCanvas functionality including selection, movement, connection, and state management
missing_requirements.reason: The AttackPathCanvas component is not present in the diff, making it impossible to verify that the six new icon types are properly handled in selection, movement, connection, save/load state operations, undo functionality, deletion, and multi-select operations. While AttackPathIcon.tsx and AttackPathToolbar.tsx add the new icons with drag support, the canvas-side integration logic is not visible.

hallucinated_features.description: Image assets referenced in iconMap for the six new security icon types
hallucinated_features.reason: The BuildRequest explicitly requires using Lucide React icon library exclusively for these icons and states 'Do not modify backend diagram state schema' as a constraint. However, AttackPathIcon.tsx includes mappings to generated image assets (backdoor: '/assets/generated/backdoor-virus-icon.dim_256x256.png', phishing: '/assets/generated/phishing-email-icon.dim_256x256.png', etc.) in the iconMap, even though the code correctly uses Lucide React icons for rendering. These asset references were not requested and contradict the exclusive use of Lucide React.
